codeReview.summary: The feature implementation matches the requested Tamagui layout skeleton behavior (desktop two-column layout, responsive stacking, breadcrumb chevron separator, and desktop right-panel swap placement) while preserving existing data hook wiring. One non-blocking theming consistency issue was identified.

issues.description: Chevron color is hardcoded as "rgba(255,255,255,0.38)" instead of using a theme token (e.g., $neutral3), which weakens theme consistency and conflicts with mission guidance to use tokens for styling values.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The tamagui-frontend-worker skill's mandatory 'Write Tests First (TDD)' step does not fit purely structural/layout-only migrations; the worker had to deviate and rely on computed-style visual checks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.deviations includes step 'Write Tests First (TDD)' with justification that this feature is structural layout work verified via agent-browser measurements.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Tamagui View typing does not support CSS position: sticky in this setup, leading to a plain div wrapper workaround for sticky swap placement; this limitation should be captured in shared guidance.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: web/app/page.tsx:133 uses <div style={{ position: "sticky", top: 72 }}> and handoff.discoveredIssues explicitly documents the Tamagui sticky typing limitation.

summary: Review completed for tdp-layout-skeleton (commit cf8168c). The feature is functionally complete for its scope and passes scrutiny with one non-blocking theming-token issue plus two shared-state observations for skill/knowledge documentation.
